candidates.stmt:
Pete is a Bolton-based community advocate, media creator, and accessibility campaigner with a long history of hands-on local action. His work spans housing scrutiny, environmental clean-ups, grassroots media, and speaking up for residents in spaces where their voices are often missing.

Pete served on the Bolton at Home Scrutiny Panel, contributing resident-led insight and challenge, and was one of the driving forces behind the removal of 37 tons of rubbish from Johnson Fold — a practical achievement that reflected coordination, persistence, and community cooperation rather than rhetoric.
He is the creator and online editor of the borough’s third-largest local media network, which he built from scratch.

Starting with no existing infrastructure, Pete assembled a dedicated team and developed a platform focused on amplifying resident voices, sharing local information, and holding power to account. The network prioritises lived experience over spin and has become a trusted source for community-led reporting.

Pete is disabled and autistic, and works alongside his assistance dog, Princess, who supports him in both daily life and community engagement. Accessibility, inclusion, and honesty are central to his approach — not as abstract ideals, but as lived realities that shape how communities function and who gets heard.

From community action on the ground to speaking truth to power, including challenging cabinet ministers in live settings, Pete’s contribution to Bolton has been defined by consistency, independence, and a refusal to let resident concerns be ignored.

Charlotte served for 30 years as a Police Officer, both for Greater Manchester Police – where she led a Neighbourhood Policing Team – and the Metropolitan Police Service.  
 
Charlotte is also a trustee of the charity Sex Matters, a member of the LGB Alliance Business Forum and coordinates the Women’s Rights Network in Greater Manchester. She has also been involved in local Conservative politics in Bolton and a school Governor in the area.

Labour have failed the people of this constituency, hammering the local high streets with higher business rates and local residents with higher taxes to pay for more welfare.

Meanwhile Reform and the Greens both offer nothing, but fantasy politics built on unfunded promises and socialist economics – with no serious plan to tackle the serious issues our country faces.

Only the Conservatives, under Kemi Badenoch’s leadership, have the team and the plan to deliver – and Charlotte Cadden will fight for that in Gorton and Denton.

Joe Burton is a friendly, local, and purpose-driven leader and creative with over ten years experience championing and supporting young people’s ambitions across sectors, from science to arts, health and human rights. He leads a national youth programme, volunteers locally at Bolton Lads and Girls Club and serves on the Committee for Bolton Green Party. He has also served as Chair on a Board of Directors for five years.

"Bromley Cross is a tight-knit community with a strong voice that needs to be heard. I believe this community deserves a bright future.

My priorities are simple: ensure that **everyone** in our community has a voice and is heard, improve public services, and protect our local environment. This means safeguarding green belt land, thinking clearly about road improvements, supporting sustainable transport, and investing in the local services that people rely on every day.

The Green Party offers a different vision for the future - one rooted in care for all communities and for the planet. I will stand against politics which seeks to divide us - this has no place in Bromley Cross."

Hello I’m Julie Pattison, the Reform UK parliamentary candidate for Bolton South and Walkden. I have lived in Kearsley all my life and in 2018 I was a co-founder of Farnworth and Kearsley First, which was formed by a group of residents who were fed up of being let down by our local politicians and watching a once great area fall into decline. I joined Reform UK in 2022 out of sheer frustration over the treatment of our towns by the ruling parties. We have had a Labour MP for 12 years and seen no improvement or fight.

I have strong connections in Bolton South and Walkden through work, friends and family.

My working career has always been in the care/charity sectors. Being a lifelong learner and passionate about social justice, I have a deep sense of empathy for the needs of some of the most vulnerable in society and can identify with many people who are let down under the current system. Working with grass roots has given me the ability to see first-hand the impact our government has had on working families. We have all felt the negative impact of lack of access to GP's, NHS, housing, inflation (IE tax rises, energy rises), failing education and with no sign of improvement.

Any politician that stands proud to cut the red ribbon when they are announcing another food bank opening, should hang their heads in shame. It is for these reasons why I joined Reform, as I worry about what kind of future my grandchildren will have.

I am not a career politician; I am just a working-class lass from the North. I am doing this because I want better for our towns and future generations.

If I am blessed to be gifted your vote I promise to fight for improvement and be your voice.

I am a lecturer in biological sciences at Greater Manchester Medical school, helping to make this development the success it deserves to be for its students, for Farnworth, and for Bolton. I want to put the skills I have learnt in a career that has taken me to Africa, to Central Asia, and to China to use in improving Farnworth and Bolton.

I hold the Green values of environment, equality, and education to be vital to the development of places that strengthen the people that live in them in body, mind, and community. So that health and wellbeing come naturally not after a struggle against the way we are forced to live.

Communities that value everyone within them wherever they were born, whatever they believe, and whatever struggles they have overcome and continue to face. A society that enables all those within it to make the most of their own talents and abilities throughout their life.

Having lived in Horwich for nearly 25years I have had the pleasure of being the longest serving HBFI councillor at Bolton, helping to make sure Horwich and Blackrod are treated with fairness when it comes to investing in our communities.

If I am elected, my first three priorities will be to provide young people with opportunities and places to go, and transform our public services.

1. Secure long-term funding for youth services in Kearsley
2. Bring public services back to public ownership
3. Overhaul our waste service to put an end to fly tipping and dumping

Ben is a high school teacher and Head of Year, and has lived in Little Lever for the past six years, with strong family ties to the area. Through his work in education, Ben understands the challenges facing young people and families. He is passionate about improving support for schools, particularly SEND provision, and ensuring all young people have access to the opportunities and extracurricular activities they need to thrive.

Ben is actively involved in the community and recently took part in Bolton Hospice’s Strictly Learn to Dance 2026. He and his husband, who manages a local pub, are passionate about the area and committed to helping it thrive.

Ben is standing for the Green Party because he believes in fairness, inclusion, and bringing communities together. He wants to build on the work already done locally while offering a fresh, positive vision focused on community pride, safety, and opportunity for all.

His priorities include strengthening education and support for young people, improving and protecting local green spaces, and boosting community life through investment in high streets and local facilities. He also wants to ensure that new development is properly planned, with the infrastructure and transport needed to support it.

I plan to help my local community thrive. People deserve the opportunity have a clean and prosperous environment and I will do my best to achieve that goal.

I will focus on cleaning streets, fixing roads to reduce accidents and improve public transport links. I will work with community groups to make sure they thrive and I will make sure the interests of the people are communicated.

Social justice is a top priority for me, all people deserve respect regardless of background gender or faith. I am an engineer by trade so I know how to plan and achieve results effectively, I will use these skills to do all I can to achieve my goals.

I am a long term resident of Smithills.  I am a retired science teacher with many years of experience as a manager in a large secondary school. This means I have the skills to work with people and get things done.

Hello I’m Trevor Jones the Reform UK Parliamentary Candidate for Bolton North East.

I’m a true Boltonian who was born, raised and have lived here all of my life.

I’m a self-employed builder who has run my own building company in Bolton for over 40 years.

If you give me the privilege / honour of representing you in Parliament then I will give you my word that has your MP I will fight in Westminster for your rights, liberty and freedom to improve your standard of living like no previous Bolton MP has ever fought for his Constituents and that’s a promise.

Hi, I'm Gaynor and I have lived in Tonge with the Haulgh ward for 14 years, and I care passionately about the local community and its people. I believe that Bolton needs positive change that works for the benefit of all residents regardless of their background and the Green Party is the only Party that can offer this. Fundamentally, I know that Boltonians are kind and caring people who have often been let down by our political system. Over the years I have watched with dismay as various council town improvement plans have failed to bring life back to Bolton centre. As a Green Party councillor, I would prioritise giving local people more of a say in how our town develops, respecting different perspectives and supporting fairness and equity for all.

**As a representative of Bolton Green Party, I promise to:**
- consult with residents regularly to explore local issues and concerns
- provide feedback to residents on my actions to address local issues
- focus on ensuring our local environments are safe, clean and sustainable
- work to bring our varied communities closer together socially and professionally

I live in the ward with my partner and two sons (the youngest is just a month old so I've not been able to be as visible as some as the other candidates lately). This is part of the reason I am so passionate about helping my friends and neighbours make our community the best it can be.

I am a Liberal Democrat because one of their key values is that of empowering local communities. It's not about some faceless politician you've never seen making decisions about an area they may never have visited. I believe in helping people to help themselves so that the right support can be received in the right areas. 

Littering/fly tipping, crime and speeding are some of the key issues that residents have raised to me across the ward. 

Supporting residents with waste disposal is something I am keen to implement as a councillor for our area - I have several ideas around this which I would look to implement, one being a council approved additional waste collector scheme so that residents can have confidence that their waste is being collected and not fly tipped by the waste removal company.

I have personally experienced difficulties in contacting the local neighbourhood officers in relation to local crime issues and I would look to ensure a more open platform for communication with our local police neighbourhood teams. Working together will make the approach to policing more relevant and have a a more positive impact on the relationship between the police and our community.

It was with great sadness that I saw the speed watch scheme, set up by volunteers in the ward, dissolved due to a lack of support. As your local councillor, I would work to try and ensure there is support for such schemes from the police and council as this was such a positive example of local residents taking direct action to improve their own area.

This is just a taste of some of my thoughts on how I could support the community to improve our ward and if there is a specific issue you would like to know m

I am a passionate advocate for fairness and treating others with respect. I have been volunteering in Bolton since childhood to make our town a better place for everyone. Through my experiences over the last decade, I have developed many skills that would make me a good Councillor for Westhoughton North and Hunger Hill. I have experience in:
- Committee work
- Public Speaking
- Project management 
- Data analysis 
- Communicating with community members

I am about to graduate from university with a degree in Social Policy and Quantitative Research Methods. With my policy degree and suddenly wide-open availability, I have a lot to give to the role of councillor.

My candidacy will offer a positive option for those looking to vote for community cohesion and mutual respect.

Westhoughton North and Hunger Hill deserves a councillor who genuinely cares about the people and community spaces they serve. If trusted with this responsibility, I would not disappoint.

I'm David Wilkinson, your Liberal Democrat candidate in Westhoughton South, and I've lived in Westhoughton my entire life. This is my home — and that's exactly why I'm standing.

I'm deeply concerned about the over-development that is threatening the character of our town. Too many homes are being approved without the infrastructure to support them, and residents are already feeling the consequences: you can't get a GP appointment when you need one, families are struggling to secure the school place of their choice, and roads like Park Road are now at a standstill. I'll fight to make sure Westhoughton's voice is heard when decisions are made about our land and our streets.

I'm also campaigning to turn Westhoughton Town Hall into a health centre for our growing community. As our population increases, access to local health services matters more than ever — and this landmark building could be at the heart of that.

Many of us are dismayed at what Labour has and hasn't delivered for Westhoughton. Our town still lacks the investment and attention it deserves. But this election is closer than you might think — I need just 117 votes to stop Labour being returned for another four years.

If you want a councillor who knows Westhoughton, loves Westhoughton, and will work tirelessly for the people who live here — I'd be honoured to have your vote on 7 May.

Simon is corporate fundraiser for a national charity dedicated to supporting employment, justice services, education, young people and families. With a background in youth work, previously managing national citizen service programmes, supporting young people across Greater Manchester to develop independence, team working and employability and life skills.

Simon’s political awakening began in 2010 when he attended a march in London to protest against university fees being tripled. Since then he has been a passionate advocate for progressive politics believing we can bring about change when we work in solidarity.

"I look forward to campaigning to protect the green space around Westhoughton, push for measures to improve road congestion and take down the cheap, often damaged flags put up around town that have become an eyesore and don’t represent our welcoming community.”

Jack Speight is a farm manager, and has served as Independent Councillor since 2012. Passionate about protecting Westhoughton's heritage, community and preserving our rural landscape and greenbelt from the wanton development that’s destroying our identity and way of life. Advocate and supporter of local businesses, a strong local economy, protection of key services, and fair share of Bolton Council expenditure for Westhoughton and its residents.

Jack is the Westhoughton First Independents candidate for Borough Councillor for the Westhoughton North Ward in the Bolton Council Elections. He is also seeking re-election to Westhoughton Town Council for the Westhoughton Chequerbent Ward.

LET'S PUT WESTHOUGHTON FIRST

I’m standing as an Independent candidate for Westhoughton South with a clear set of priorities focused on what really matters to residents:

- Value for money – greater transparency and accountability to stop wasteful spending

- Fix our roads properly – no more short-term patches, tackle potholes for good

- Safer streets – work with police to reduce anti-social behaviour

- Stronger community voice – make sure residents are heard and involved

- Cleaner, greener neighbourhoods – tackle fly-tipping and improve street cleaning

- Better spaces for children – safe, modern play areas for families

This is about focusing on the basics, listening to residents, and delivering real improvements for our town.

Your voice. Your money. Your Westhoughton.

🗳 Vote Independent on 7th May.
